title: ReceivedIpv6::sender_iid uses raw [u8; 8] instead of NodeId newtype
description:
lichen-core defines NodeId([u8; 8]) for EUI-64 identifiers. However:

lichen-node/src/stack.rs ReceivedIpv6 struct:
  pub struct ReceivedIpv6 {
      pub sender_iid: [u8; 8],  // raw array
      ...
  }

NodeId is used elsewhere in the crate:
- Node::new(node_id: NodeId)
- Stack::node_id() -> NodeId
- RplNode::new(node_id: NodeId, ...)

What goes wrong: The raw [u8; 8] cannot use NodeId::link_local_addr() or other methods. Callers must manually convert via NodeId(sender_iid) to use the newtype's functionality. This defeats the purpose of having a newtype.
